quiz.title: Unit 8 Quiz 7, Grade 10 Biology
quiz.subtitle: Protists and Parasites: Diversity, Classification, and Disease
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Protist Classification, Plant-like and Animal-like Protists, Protozoan Diseases, Malaria, Trypanosoma, Euglena, Ectoparasites vs Endoparasites, Trematodes

questions.question: Protists are primarily:
questions.explanation: Protists are eukaryotic organisms that are not plants, animals, or fungi. They can be unicellular, colonial, or simple multicellular. They have membrane-bound organelles and a true nucleus.

questions.question: Which of the following is a plant-like protist?
questions.explanation: Euglena is a plant-like protist because it contains chloroplasts and performs photosynthesis. Amoeba and Paramecium are animal-like protists (protozoa), and Plasmodium is a parasitic protozoan.

questions.question: Which of the following is an animal-like protist?
questions.explanation: Amoeba is an animal-like protist (protozoan) that moves and feeds by pseudopods. Diatoms are plant-like protists (algae), Spirogyra is a green alga, and Volvox is a colonial alga.

questions.question: Malaria is caused by which protist?
questions.explanation: Malaria is caused by Plasmodium species (P. falciparum, P. vivax, etc.), transmitted by Anopheles mosquitoes. Trypanosoma causes sleeping sickness, Giardia causes giardiasis, and Paramecium is a free-living protist.

questions.question: Protists can reproduce by:
questions.explanation: Protists exhibit both asexual reproduction (binary fission, multiple fission) and sexual reproduction (conjugation, gamete formation). This allows them to adapt to changing environmental conditions.

questions.question: Trypanosoma causes:
questions.explanation: Trypanosoma brucei causes African sleeping sickness, transmitted by tsetse flies. Dysentery can be caused by Entamoeba, malaria by Plasmodium, and influenza by virus.

questions.question: Which protist moves using cilia?
questions.explanation: Paramecium moves using cilia (short, hair-like structures). Amoeba uses pseudopods, Plasmodium has no specialized locomotion (transmitted by mosquitoes), and Giardia uses flagella.

questions.question: Euglena is unique because it:
questions.explanation: Euglena is mixotrophic - it can perform photosynthesis (autotrophic) when light is available and absorb nutrients (heterotrophic) when light is absent. It is unicellular, eukaryotic, and does not cause malaria.

questions.question: Parasites that complete their life cycle in one host are called:
questions.explanation: Monoxenous (or direct) parasites complete their entire life cycle in a single host. Examples include Ascaris (roundworm) and Enterobius (pinworm). Heteroxenous parasites require multiple hosts (e.g., Plasmodium needs humans and mosquitoes). Endoparasites live inside the host, ectoparasites live outside—these terms describe location, not host number.

questions.question: Protists are classified as:
questions.explanation: Protists are broadly classified into three groups: protozoa (animal-like), algae (plant-like), and slime molds/water molds (fungus-like). They are not bacteria, viruses, or true fungi.

questions.question: Which of the following is an ectoparasite?
questions.explanation: Ticks are ectoparasites that live on the external surface of hosts and feed on blood. Plasmodium, Trypanosoma, and Giardia are endoparasites that live inside the host's body.

questions.question: Which of the following is an endoparasite?
questions.explanation: Tapeworm is an endoparasite that lives inside the host's intestines. Fleas, lice, and mosquitoes are ectoparasites (external parasites).

questions.question: Malaria parasites are transmitted by:
questions.explanation: Plasmodium (malaria parasite) is transmitted by female Anopheles mosquitoes. Ticks transmit diseases like Lyme disease, fleas transmit plague, and lice transmit typhus.

questions.question: Trypanosoma brucei is transmitted by:
questions.explanation: Trypanosoma brucei, which causes African sleeping sickness, is transmitted by tsetse flies (Glossina species). Anopheles transmits malaria, sandflies transmit leishmaniasis.

questions.question: Schistosoma is a:
questions.explanation: Schistosoma is a trematode (flatworm/parasitic fluke) causing schistosomiasis. It is not a protozoan (single-celled), virus, or fungus. It has a complex life cycle involving snails as intermediate hosts.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered protist classification and parasitic diseases! 🦠
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the differences between plant-like and animal-like protists.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on disease transmission and parasite types.
scoreMessages.0: Please review protist characteristics, protozoan diseases, and parasite classifications.
